title: Null rebalancing-state mock is cleared instead of installed
reasoning: The helper is documented as pre-setting a mock rebalancing state, and other RPC helpers preserve null with a sentinel or a map entry so fetchers return null for simulated RPC failure. Here, passing null deletes the in-memory mock, so direct unit tests using fetchRebalancingState fall through to the configured RPC client instead of exercising the failure path. That can make a test accidentally pass or hit live/test HTTP behavior rather than the intended null branch.
reproduction: Call _setMockRebalancingState(chainId, pool, null), install a test RPC client whose readContract returns a valid getRebalancingState tuple, then call fetchRebalancingState(chainId, pool, block). It returns the valid tuple instead of null because the null mock was deleted.
recommendation: Store a null sentinel in _testRebalancingStates, matching the reserves helper pattern, or store null directly and keep has() as the discriminator. Clear the map only in _clearMockRebalancingStates.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise BiPoolManager, breaker probing, and invertRateFeed effect decoding, but none call _setMockRebalancingState with null and then fetchRebalancingState directly.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a pool-state RPC test that sets _setMockRebalancingState(CHAIN_ID, pool, null), installs a readContract client that would otherwise return a valid tuple, and asserts fetchRebalancingState returns null without consuming the fallback value.
minimumFixScope: indexer-envio/src/rpc/pool-state.ts plus one focused unit test for the null mock contract

history.note: Fixed by storing null as an installed rebalancing-state mock and adding a no-RPC-fallthrough regression.
